understanding.goal_understanding: Navigate the currently selected piece (and potentially other selectable pieces) around the grid to reach the goal tile(s) marked with '+', within the available step budget for the level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Positions were given as pixel coordinates [x, y] with a 3-pixels-per-cell grid; MOVE actions attempted to shift the selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction, but could result in no movement when blocked by walls/boundaries or other objects. Some adjacent objects (e.g., blocks) were pushable and would move along if pushed in the movement direction, subject to whatever blocked them. Walls ('#' and other boundaries) could prevent movement, though the movement.{direction}_blocked flag was only a heuristic that indicated an adjacent object and did not guarantee failure. Levels could contain multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK on a selectable's exact [x, y] position switched control to that piece (as seen when clicking between [15, 21], [21, 21], and later [36, 21]).
